name: express-rate-limit
what_it_does: Rate limiting middleware for Express.js APIs. express-rate-limit counts requests per IP (or custom key) within a time window and returns 429 Too Many Requests when limits are exceeded. Supports in-memory store (single process) and external stores (Redis, Memcached) for distributed rate limiting across multiple agent API instances.
use_cases: Protect agent API endpoints from brute-force attacks and abuse with per-IP rate limiting
Implement tiered rate limits for agent APIs — higher limits for authenticated users, lower for anonymous
Rate limit expensive LLM-calling endpoints in agent backends to control costs and prevent abuse
Add rate limiting to agent login/auth endpoints to prevent credential stuffing attacks
Apply different rate limits to different agent API routes (strict on auth, lenient on static data)
not_for: Distributed rate limiting at scale — in-memory store only works for single processes; use Redis store for multiple instances
Application-level quotas (monthly billing limits) — use dedicated quota management solutions
best_when: You need simple, configurable rate limiting middleware for Express.js agent APIs with minimal setup.
avoid_when: You need distributed rate limiting across multiple processes without Redis, or you're using Fastify.

auth.notes: Rate limiting middleware — no auth. Use with Passport or JWT auth to enable user-specific limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header and configurable error message. RateLimitInfo object available in response for client consumption.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Rate limit state is in memory or Redis — deterministic counting per window.
known_agent_gotchas: Default in-memory store doesn't work across multiple Node.js processes — multi-instance deployments require Redis store (rate-limit-redis package)
IP detection behind proxies: must set app.set('trust proxy', 1) for correct IP when behind load balancer/nginx — otherwise rate limits apply to proxy IP
windowMs vs max: rate limiting applies max requests per windowMs — these must be tuned together; low max with long window blocks legitimate usage
Rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-*, Retry-After) are sent by default — ensure clients use these to implement backoff; agents should respect Retry-After
skip option can bypass rate limiting for trusted IPs or authenticated users — whitelist internal agent-to-agent calls to avoid rate limiting your own services
Memory store grows unbounded for unique IPs — in high-traffic scenarios with many unique IPs, set max window to limit memory accumulation

security_score_components.security_notes: Rate limiting is a security control — configure appropriately tight limits for auth endpoints. Combine with Helmet for comprehensive HTTP security.
